seed_request.text_input: Our margins are down in Q3. We need to know what to do next.
seed_request.voice_transcript: Our margins are down in Q3. What should we do?
seed_request.context_notes: Leadership wants a concrete plan in under three weeks.

intake.problem_statement: The business has a Q3 margin compression issue and needs a scoped recommendation on where to act first.
extracted_signals: Potential mix of pricing, COGS, and SKU portfolio issues
assumptions: The margin issue is concentrated in a subset of products rather than the entire portfolio
The team can implement operational changes within one quarter
missing_information: Cost center detail for procurement and manufacturing
clarifying_questions: Which product lines are driving the largest margin decline?
Is the primary goal cost reduction, price optimization, or portfolio rationalization?
What margin target does leadership expect to recover?

clarify.problem_statement: Determine how Signal2Action should diagnose and reverse Q3 margin compression for the most impacted product line.
clarify.clarified_scope: Focus the analysis on Product Line A, quantify the biggest cost and mix drivers, and propose near-term actions to recover margin.
assumptions: Product Line A is the most material driver of Q3 margin compression
The company can influence supplier terms and SKU mix within the next quarter
missing_information: SKU-level profitability for Product Line A
Supplier concentration and contract renewal dates
clarifying_questions: Should the plan prioritize speed of recovery or longer-term margin durability?
Are there strategic SKUs that cannot be discontinued even if margins are poor?
What budget exists for operational changes or analytics support?

review.approved_scope: Confirmed scope: focus on Product Line A COGS reduction and SKU portfolio cleanup, with a target to recover 4 points of gross margin within two quarters.
review_notes: Human reviewer confirmed that pricing redesign is out of scope for this demo and wants the plan centered on operational levers.

current_state: Margin visibility is fragmented across finance and operations reports
Supplier spend is concentrated in three contracts with limited negotiation cadence
SKU reporting does not clearly separate high-volume from high-margin products
constraints: The team needs an executive-ready recommendation within three weeks
Major ERP changes are out of scope for the initial action plan
Pricing changes require a separate commercial workstream
dependencies: Finance must provide a clean margin bridge by SKU
Procurement needs renewal timing for top supplier contracts
Operations needs owner assignment for weekly margin tracking
gaps: No consistent cost attribution layer by product line
Two underperforming SKUs consume disproportionate working capital
Supplier negotiations are reactive instead of planned

modules.name: Cost Attribution Layer
modules.objective: Create a decision-ready view of margin drivers by SKU and product line.

modules.name: SKU Portfolio Analysis
modules.objective: Separate strategic SKUs from margin-dilutive SKUs and identify rationalization candidates.

modules.name: Supplier Negotiation Sprint
modules.objective: Quantify contract renegotiation opportunities across top suppliers.

external_context.title: Peer Margin Benchmark Snapshot
external_context.summary: Comparable firms recovered 2-5 margin points by reducing long-tail SKUs and renegotiating concentrated supplier spend.

external_context.title: Commodity Pressure Note
external_context.summary: Recent commodity inflation affected input costs, but category leaders offset it with targeted procurement programs.

plan.summary: Start with SKU profitability and supplier concentration because both can move margin within one quarter without pricing changes.

recommendations.title: Rationalize the bottom two SKUs in Product Line A
recommendations.rationale: They dilute margin, tie up working capital, and contribute limited strategic value.

recommendations.title: Renegotiate the top three supplier contracts
recommendations.rationale: Supplier concentration creates leverage and can unlock near-term COGS savings.

recommendations.title: Stand up weekly margin tracking by product line
recommendations.rationale: The organization lacks a shared operating view of margin movement and recovery progress.

tradeoffs.upside: Fastest path to margin recovery and complexity reduction.
tradeoffs.downside: May create revenue risk if stakeholders disagree on strategic SKU value.

tradeoffs.upside: Less visible to customers and commercial teams.
tradeoffs.recommendation_bias: Useful as a partial move, not sufficient alone.

action_plan.action: Build SKU profitability view and confirm discontinuation candidates.
Prepare and launch negotiation briefs for top three suppliers.
action_plan.outcome: Negotiation plan with target savings by supplier.

action_plan.action: Launch weekly margin tracking cadence for Product Line A.
action_plan.outcome: Operating dashboard tied to savings and recovery targets.

checks: Handled mixed text and voice inputs
Preserved human review checkpoint before assessment
Produced structured outputs for all workflow stages
risks: Real-world ERP data quality may reduce confidence in SKU-level analysis
Supplier negotiation timing depends on contract renewal windows

seed_request.text_input: We think Southeast Asia might be our next market. Is that the right move?
seed_request.voice_transcript: Should we expand into Southeast Asia now?
seed_request.context_notes: The team needs a first-market recommendation, not a full regional strategy.

intake.problem_statement: The business needs a fact-based recommendation on whether Southeast Asia should be the next expansion move.
extracted_signals: Leadership is uncertain about geographic prioritization
A market choice must translate into a concrete launch plan
assumptions: Localization capacity is not yet mature
clarifying_questions: Is the goal market selection, launch planning, or both?
How much localization is feasible in the next 90 days?

clarify.problem_statement: Decide whether Southeast Asia is the right next market and identify the best first entry point.
clarify.clarified_scope: Rank candidate markets, choose one first launch country, and define a 90-day validation plan.
assumptions: One pilot market is sufficient for initial validation
clarifying_questions: Should the pilot optimize for revenue potential or operational ease?
What local compliance steps are mandatory?

review.approved_scope: Confirmed scope: choose one launch market in Southeast Asia and outline a 90-day pilot GTM plan.
review_notes: Human reviewer deferred long-term hiring and org design decisions.

modules.name: Country Screening
modules.objective: Rank launch markets by attractiveness and ease.

modules.name: Pilot GTM Design
modules.objective: Define how the first-market pilot should be run.

external_context.title: SEA SaaS Market Snapshot
external_context.summary: Singapore offers faster validation and lower operational drag despite lower TAM than Indonesia.

plan.summary: Pilot in Singapore first, then widen based on channel and CAC evidence.

recommendations.title: Launch the first pilot in Singapore
recommendations.rationale: It balances ease of execution, talent access, and partnership readiness.

tradeoffs.downside: Higher complexity and slower feedback loops.
tradeoffs.recommendation_bias: Not recommended for the first pilot.

action_plan.action: Finalize country scorecard and partner shortlist.

seed_request.text_input: Our onboarding process is slowing down and customers are frustrated.
seed_request.voice_transcript: Our onboarding is slow. Fix it.
seed_request.context_notes: The VP wants a 30/60/90-day operations plan.

intake.problem_statement: The business needs to diagnose onboarding slowdown and translate it into an action plan that improves time-to-value.
assumptions: The bottleneck is process-driven rather than purely staffing-driven
clarifying_questions: Which onboarding stage has the highest delay?

clarify.problem_statement: Reduce onboarding delays and improve customer time-to-value.
clarify.clarified_scope: Focus on handoff design, approvals, and milestone visibility across onboarding.
clarifying_questions: Should the first release focus on enterprise customers only?

review.approved_scope: Confirmed scope: improve onboarding workflow and reduce time-to-value by 30%.
review_notes: Human reviewer excluded pricing and support staffing changes.

modules.name: Handoff Redesign
modules.objective: Remove operational friction between sales, CS, and implementation.

external_context.title: B2B onboarding benchmark
external_context.summary: Best-in-class onboarding teams centralize ownership and expose milestone visibility to customers.

plan.summary: Start with ownership and approval routing before automation-heavy work.

action_plan.action: Redesign handoff workflow and unify intake packet.

risks: Staffing may still be a latent bottleneck
